steps.title: Identify what direct support requires
steps.content: To directly support that invasives are advantaged by delayed subfreezing onset, we need evidence that the invasive L. densiflorum gains more from the extended warm period than the native O. secunda does.

steps.title: Apply this to the choices
steps.content:
Choice B shows that when subfreezing temperatures are delayed, L. densiflorum extends its leaf-production period far more than O. secunda does, directly demonstrating the invasive's advantage.

The other choices either fail to show the invasive gaining advantage (A shows it stopping sooner; D shows no variation in response) or muddy the mechanism with irrelevant variables (C).

That matches choice B — B is correct.
